an5.161:1.1: “There are these five ways of subduing hatred by which, when hatred arises in a monk, he should wipe it out completely.
an5.161:1.3: “When you give birth to hatred for an individual, you should develop goodwill for that individual.
an5.161:1.4: Thus the hatred for that individual should be subdued.
an5.161:1.5: “When you give birth to hatred for an individual, you should develop compassion for that individual.
an5.161:1.6: Thus the hatred for that individual should be subdued.
an5.161:1.7: “When you give birth to hatred for an individual, you should develop equanimity toward that individual.
an5.161:1.8: Thus the hatred for that individual should be subdued.
an5.161:1.9: “When you give birth to hatred for an individual, you should pay him no mind & pay him no attention.
an5.161:1.10: Thus the hatred for that individual should be subdued.
an5.161:1.11: “When you give birth to hatred for an individual, you should direct your thoughts to the fact of his being the product of his actions:
an5.161:1.12: ‘This venerable one is the doer of his actions, heir of his actions, born of his actions, related by his actions, and has his actions as his arbitrator.
an5.161:1.13: Whatever action he does, for good or for evil, to that will he fall heir.’
an5.161:1.14: Thus the hatred for that individual should be subdued.
an5.161:1.15: “These are five ways of subduing hatred by which, when hatred arises in a monk, he should wipe it out completely.”
